places.name: Albert 1er
places.near: In Chamonix town, ~10-min walk north of the centre toward Le Bouchet; taxi or drive, parking on site.
places.order: The tasting menu; Lake Geneva Arctic char and whitefish, Mont-Blanc-area snails, local pork/lamb, and produce from the herb garden.
places.notes: 1 MICHELIN star retained for 2026; head chef Damien Leveau.
places.famousFor: The valley's flagship gastronomic restaurant — 1 MICHELIN star, inside the 5-star Relais & Châteaux Hameau Albert 1er, with a legendary 120-year cellar.
places.goodToKnow: Dinner only, ~19:15–21:00, roughly Fri–Tue (check current days); reservations essential; smart dress; high-end pricing. A special-occasion/couples pick.

places.name: Auberge du Bois Prin
places.area: Les Moussoux (above Chamonix, toward Le Brévent)
places.near: In the quiet Les Moussoux hamlet on the Brévent side; short drive/taxi up from Chamonix centre; parking on site.
places.order: The multi-course tasting menu (around 7 steps); dishes built on herbs, fruit and vegetables from the kitchen garden.
places.notes: 1 MICHELIN star; Emmanuel Renaut partnership since 2020, resident chef Quentin Veyrat.
places.famousFor: 1 MICHELIN star; nature-inspired cuisine associated with 3-star chef Emmanuel Renaut, in a refined chalet with sweeping Mont Blanc views and alpine gardens.
places.goodToKnow: Typically open Wed–Sun; reserve well ahead (a few weeks in peak season); one of the most romantic Mont Blanc views in the valley — couples/luxury.

places.name: La Maison Carrier
places.near: In Chamonix town toward Le Bouchet, ~10-min walk north of centre; parking on site.
places.order: Cheese fondue and raclette, wood-fired grilled meats in winter, croûte; summer terrace hors-d'oeuvres buffet facing Mont Blanc.
places.famousFor: Refined regional cooking in a rebuilt Savoyard farmhouse — the accessible, tradition-focused sister to Albert 1er. A benchmark for well-made fondue and raclette.
places.goodToKnow: Family-friendly yet polished; open lunch and dinner on operating days (seasonal schedule — verify); reservations recommended. Great for a quality Savoyard night.

places.name: Le Matafan (Hôtel Mont-Blanc)
places.near: Central Chamonix, off Allée du Majestic; walkable from anywhere in town.
places.order: Menu changes every ~2 weeks; look for Lyon-style truffled sausage, barbecued beef chuck, pigeon with endive and ginger broth.
places.famousFor: The elegant restaurant of the 5-star Hôtel Mont-Blanc, run by Michelin-starred chef Mickey Bourdillat; polished cooking in an interior designed by Sybille de Margerie.
places.goodToKnow: Open daily in season (winter ~late Nov–late Apr, summer ~May–Nov); reservations advised; luxury/couples, but more relaxed than a starred temple.

places.name: Le Bistrot
places.area: Chamonix town centre (near Hôtel Le Morgane)
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue Docteur Paccard; walkable.
places.order: The regularly changing market menu; well-matched seasonal dishes using local, Swiss and Italian valley produce.
places.notes: Founding chef Mickey Bourdillat moved to Le Matafan; confirm current chef/menu when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A long-standing bistronomic address (formerly Michelin-starred under Mickey Bourdillat) known for refined, accessible cooking and a strong regional wine list.
places.goodToKnow: Dinner-focused (from ~19:00); newly renovated modern interior; reservations recommended. A refined date-night/couples spot.

places.name: Le Cap Horn
places.cuisine: Modern French, seafood & sushi with Asian accents
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue des Moulins; take the stairs up from street level to the upper floors.
places.order: Fresh seafood platters, the sushi menu, and the memorable desserts; extensive wine list.
places.famousFor: Elegant, nautical-themed dining above one of Chamonix's oldest streets; seafood-forward menu and a notably popular sushi selection.
places.goodToKnow: Warm wooden interior, elegant atmosphere; higher-end mains; reservations recommended. Good for couples or a special dinner; ranked ~#26 on Tripadvisor with ~1,980 reviews.

places.name: Le Monchu
places.area: Chamonix town centre (Rue des Moulins area)
places.near: Central Chamonix, near Rue des Moulins; walkable.
places.order: Fondue and raclette (generous portions), croûte, tartiflette; three-course Tradition menu; kids' menu available.
places.notes: Confirm exact street number when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A Chamonix institution since 1972 for cheese specialities in a cozy wood-clad chalet setting over two levels — a classic family fondue-and-raclette night.
places.goodToKnow: Very family-friendly with alcoves for groups; busy in season, book ahead; reviews mostly strong though service can slow when full.

places.name: La Calèche
places.area: Chamonix town centre (Rue du Docteur Paccard)
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue du Docteur Paccard; walkable.
places.order: Fondue, raclette and tartiflette; regional French mains (~€20–€35).
places.notes: Verify website URL when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: One of the most atmospheric traditional restaurants in town — antiques, old wood and alpine bric-a-brac make it very photogenic; a reliable classic for Savoyard fare.
places.goodToKnow: Lively atmosphere (sometimes live music); popular with families and groups; reservations recommended for dinner.

places.name: Le 3842
places.area: Summit of the Aiguille du Midi (3,842 m)
places.cuisine: Alpine French with Savoyard & Italian produce
places.near: Aiguille du Midi cable car from Chamonix centre (~20 min); the restaurant is at the summit terrace level.
places.order: The short seasonal à l'ardoise menu built on Savoyard and Italian mountain produce; go for the experience and the panorama.
places.famousFor: One of the highest restaurants in Europe — an intimate table at 3,842 m with jaw-dropping views over the Vallée Blanche, Géant Glacier and Mont Blanc massif. Ultra-Instagrammable.
places.goodToKnow: Lunch only (winter ~12:00–14:00, summer ~11:30–15:30); very few seats — book online well ahead; requires the (paid) cable-car ticket; altitude can affect appetite. Bucket-list.
places.address: Sommet de l'Aiguille du Midi, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Refuge du Plan de l'Aiguille
places.area: Plan de l'Aiguille (~2,207 m), Aiguille du Midi mid-station
places.near: Aiguille du Midi cable car to the Plan de l'Aiguille mid-station, then ~10–20 min walk; also reachable on foot via the Grand Balcon Nord.
places.order: Tarte aux myrtilles and the other fruit tarts (raspberry, lemon-kiwi, pear); hearty homemade alpine dishes and soup.
places.notes: Verify website and seasonal opening. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A rustic sun-trap terrace with huge views over the Aiguilles Rouges and the valley — and arguably the best tarte aux myrtilles (blueberry tart) in Chamonix (run by a former pâtissier).
places.goodToKnow: Summer/hiking season; cash-friendly mountain hut vibe; a fantastic family or couples lunch stop mid-hike; warm hospitality noted in reviews.

places.name: Le Panoramique (Montenvers – Mer de Glace)
places.area: Montenvers (1,913 m), above the Mer de Glace glacier
places.near: Montenvers rack railway (red train) from Chamonix (~20 min, 1,035 m → 1,913 m); restaurant is at the arrival station.
places.order: Slow-cooked traditional French dishes; go for a leisurely lunch with the glacier view.
places.famousFor: A light-filled dining room and vast terrace facing the Drus and the Mer de Glace glacier, reached by the historic little red Montenvers train. Spectacular, family-friendly setting.
places.goodToKnow: Combine with the Mer de Glace ice cave and train ride (great for families); reserve for lunch; requires train ticket. Also on-site: the simpler Refuge du Montenvers restaurant.

places.name: Refuge du Montenvers
places.area: Montenvers (1,913 m), above the Mer de Glace
places.near: Montenvers red train from Chamonix (~20 min); on-site at the station complex.
places.order: Savoyard specialities and traditional mountain plates; sunny-terrace lunch.
places.famousFor: A characterful high-altitude refuge dining room and sunny terrace facing the Drus, in the historic 1880 Montenvers building beside the glacier.
places.goodToKnow: More casual/authentic than Le Panoramique; pairs naturally with the Mer de Glace excursion; family-friendly.

places.name: La Bergerie de Planpraz
places.area: Planpraz (~2,000 m), Le Brévent ski/hiking area
places.near: Planpraz gondola from Chamonix centre; restaurant is at the Planpraz mid-station area.
places.order: Wood-fired grilled meats and Savoyard specialities; local-produce dishes with Savoie/Italian flavours.
places.famousFor: A handsome wooden chalet at 2,000 m facing the Mont Blanc massif, with a central wood-burning fire and a panoramic terrace — a scenic on-mountain lunch.
places.goodToKnow: Open in the lift-operating seasons; reserve ahead (limited seats); on the pricier side for a mountain restaurant — go for the view and setting.

places.name: Le Panoramic (Le Brévent summit)
places.near: Planpraz gondola then the Brévent cable car from Chamonix centre; restaurant at the summit.
places.order: Salads, local potato-and-cheese dishes, burgers and desserts — a casual lunch for the view.
places.famousFor: A circular summit terrace at 2,525 m directly across from Mont Blanc — one of the best face-on Mont Blanc panoramas in the valley.
places.goodToKnow: Typically open mid-June to mid-September (lift-dependent); accessible to pedestrians; great family/photo stop; casual self-service style.

places.name: L'Adret de la Flégère
places.near: La Flégère cable car from Les Praz de Chamonix; restaurant near the Flégère station.
places.order: Savoyard and French mountain dishes; terrace lunch with the massif view.
places.notes: (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A bright, pleasant on-mountain restaurant on the Flégère side with a terrace looking straight across to the Mont Blanc massif and the Grand Balcon Sud.
places.goodToKnow: Lift-season opening; good stop for hikers on the Grand Balcon Sud; family-friendly; verify current operator/hours.

places.name: Les Écuries de Charamillon
places.area: Le Tour / Charamillon (Balme ski area, top of the valley)
places.near: Charamillon gondola from Le Tour (top of the Chamonix valley); at the Charamillon area.
places.order: Savoyard classics and hearty mountain plates; sunny-terrace lunch.
places.notes: (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Frequently cited among the best mountain restaurants in the valley — a warm, characterful spot beneath the Autannes chairlift at the far end of the valley above Le Tour.
places.goodToKnow: Lift-season opening; family-friendly; combine with skiing/hiking in the gentle Balme area; reserve in peak times.
places.address: Domaine de Balme, Le Tour, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: JOIA by Pizzeria des Moulins
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue des Moulins; walkable.
places.order: Neapolitan pizzas (light, blistered crust), burrata, cured-meat toppings; calzone; good-value wine list.
places.notes: Confirm exact street number when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Widely rated the best pizza in Chamonix — a real wood-fired oven and a Naples pizzaiolo turning out true Neapolitan pies; cozy, familial room with an open view of the oven.
places.goodToKnow: Very family-friendly and lively; gluten-free options available; busy in season — book for dinner. Ranked ~#20 on Tripadvisor (~1,100 reviews).

places.name: Casa Valerio
places.near: Central Chamonix; walkable.
places.order: Wood-fired pizzas (big menu), fresh pasta dishes; Italian wines.
places.notes: Verify exact address when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A long-running, genuinely Italian address in Chamonix known for a huge choice of around 50 pizzas plus fresh pasta — a dependable crowd-pleaser for families.
places.goodToKnow: Family-friendly and generous; popular with locals; reservations helpful at peak times.

places.name: Munchie
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue des Moulins; walkable.
places.order: Sushi, Thai/Asian curries, wok dishes; creative sharing plates.
places.famousFor: A long-established, stylish Chamonix favourite for creative Asian-fusion cooking — sushi, curries and woks, beautifully plated; a fun change from cheese-heavy Savoyard nights.
places.goodToKnow: Trendy, intimate room; popular so book ahead for dinner; good for couples and adventurous families.

places.name: Bizes
places.near: Central Chamonix; walkable.
places.order: Josper-grilled steaks and fish; seasonal sides.
places.notes: Verify exact address/opening. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A modern grill house specialising in meat and fish cooked in a Josper charcoal oven — a quality, meat-lover's alternative to fondue.
places.goodToKnow: Good for couples/foodies who want a change from cheese; reservations recommended in season.
places.address: Bizes, Rue du Lyret, Les Plans, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Bonneville, Haute-Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France métropolitaine, 74400, France

places.name: L'Atmosphère
places.area: Chamonix town centre (riverside, by the Arve)
places.near: Central Chamonix on the riverfront (Place Balmat area); walkable.
places.order: Regional French classics; Savoyard specialities; seasonal fish and meat mains.
places.notes: Verify exact address and website when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A discreet, cozy mountain-décor bistro with veranda tables over the Arve river and a postcard view of the peaks — a reliably good, romantic town choice.
places.goodToKnow: Lovely river/mountain view from the veranda; popular for dinner — reserve; couples and families both suit it well.

places.name: La Crèmerie du Glacier
places.area: Les Bossons (les Grassonnets), just outside Chamonix toward Les Houches
places.near: In Les Bossons (~5–10 min drive/taxi from Chamonix centre toward Les Houches); near the Bossons glacier lift; parking nearby.
places.order: The signature croûte au fromage; fondue, raclette and tartiflette; charcuterie.
places.notes: Verify exact address/opening. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A hugely atmospheric old wooden chalet in the forest below the Bossons glacier, famous for its croûte au fromage and generous cheese dishes — a hidden-gem local favourite.
places.goodToKnow: Cozy, popular and small — book ahead, especially in winter; great for a characterful Savoyard evening; family-friendly.
places.address: 766 chemin de la Glacière, Les Bossons, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Le Dahu
places.near: In Argentière village (~10 min drive/bus up-valley from Chamonix, near the Grands Montets); walkable within the village.
places.order: Old-fashioned raclette and pierrade; fondue and Savoyard specialities.
places.notes: Verify exact street address. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A warm, traditional bistro in the heart of Argentière serving classic Savoyard cuisine — a cozy village alternative to the Chamonix-centre crowds.
places.goodToKnow: Good-value, family-friendly and characterful; reservations recommended in season.

places.name: Granges-en-Haute
places.near: In Les Houches (~10–15 min drive/taxi down-valley from Chamonix), near the Prarion lift; parking on site.
places.order: Refined regional dishes; Savoyard specialities; summer dining on the raised decked terrace.
places.notes: Verify exact address and website when booking. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A traditional-but-chic chalet with a wood fire and fabulous Mont Blanc massif views, a short stroll from the Prarion gondola — a stylish, hidden-gem retreat for couples.
places.goodToKnow: Cozy chic interior with fireplace in winter, terrace with massif views in summer; reservations recommended; romantic/luxury leaning.

places.name: Chalet 4810
places.near: Central Chamonix; walkable.
places.order: Hot chocolate, pastries and cakes; light savoury plates for a mid-day break.
places.notes: Verify exact address/opening. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A very pretty, stylish tea room — one of the most Instagrammable spots in town — for coffee, chocolate and delicate sweet and savoury bites.
places.goodToKnow: Great daytime/family stop and a photo spot; not a dinner restaurant. Named after Mont Blanc's height (4,810 m).
places.address: Chalet 4810, 191, Rue Joseph Vallot, Les Plans, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Bonneville, Haute-Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France métropolitaine, 74400, France

places.name: Picnic Area
places.near: Roadside picnic area in the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc valley; reachable by car, some spots near hiking trailheads
places.order: Mont Blanc and the Aiguilles with glaciers; the valley and forest foreground; alpine meadow picnic scene
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Picnic/viewpoint stop in the Chamonix valley beneath the Mont Blanc massif and its glaciers
places.goodToKnow: Clear mornings give the cleanest Mont Blanc views before afternoon cloud builds; free; summer for green meadows, winter for snow

places.name: Gare du Montenvers Mer de Glace
places.near: Montenvers station is a short walk from Chamonix town centre, next to the main SNCF railway station
places.order: The 20-minute cogwheel train ride, the Glaciorium ice museum, and the gondola plus stairs down into the man-carved Ice Cave inside the glacier
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Historic Montenvers rack railway (open since 1908) that climbs from Chamonix to a balcony overlooking the Mer de Glace, France's largest glacier
places.goodToKnow: Bring a jacket as it is much colder at altitude; the descent to the ice cave is several hundred steps, so allow extra time and energy

places.name: Chamonix Amusement Park
places.near: 5-min walk from Chamonix town centre, near the Montenvers railway station
places.order: The 1.3 km Alpine Coaster luge on rails, plus summer rides like the splash boat and giant swing
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Les Planards leisure park at the foot of Mont Blanc, home to the Chamonix Alpine Coaster luge with 540-degree spins
places.goodToKnow: Ski area in winter, amusement park April-early Nov; coaster open year-round from age 3
places.address: 351 Chem. du Pied du Grepon, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Les Chalets de Philippe
places.near: Geneva Airport (GVA) ~90 km; ~1 hr 10 min drive
places.order: Private storybook chalets with hot tubs; in-chalet dining and concierge service
places.notes: From Saved POI
places.famousFor: Cluster of antique fairytale chalets in Chamonix with Mont Blanc views
places.goodToKnow: $$$$$; ultra-romantic individual chalets

places.name: Aiguille du Midi Cable Car & Step into the Void
places.near: Departs from the Aiguille du Midi station a short walk south of Chamonix town centre; well signed, paid parking and bus stops nearby.
places.order: Round-trip adult ticket to the 3,842 m summit (approx. 83 EUR); the panoramic terrace elevator and Step into the Void are free but require an on-site timed reservation. Book a morning slot online in advance for clear skies and to guarantee your cable-car time.
places.notes: Anthropic's most famous Chamonix experience; go early to beat cloud build-up and crowds.
places.famousFor: The Chamonix icon: a vertigo-inducing cable car soaring from town to 3,842 m for face-to-face Mont Blanc panoramas, plus the glass 'Step into the Void' skywalk box suspended over a 1,000 m drop.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 season roughly 13 Jun-13 Sep (extended summer hours Jul-Aug from ~6:10 am). Altitude 3,842 m means cold, thin air and possible altitude effects even in summer - bring warm layers. Closes in poor visibility; reservations mandatory for the skywalk elevator.
places.address: 100 Place de l'Aiguille du Midi, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Panoramic Mont-Blanc Gondola (Aiguille du Midi to Pointe Helbronner)
places.area: Aiguille du Midi (France-Italy border, over the Vallée Blanche)
places.near: Boards from the top station of the Aiguille du Midi (3,842 m); reachable only via the Aiguille du Midi cable car from Chamonix.
places.order: Add-on ticket from the Aiguille du Midi summit; combine with the Skyway Monte Bianco on the Italian side for a full France-to-Italy crossing. About 30 minutes one way.
places.notes: Best paired with an early Aiguille du Midi ascent.
places.famousFor: A surreal 5 km glass-gondola glide from the Aiguille du Midi across the Géant glacier to Pointe Helbronner on the Italian side - drifting silently over crevasses and ice falls with 360-degree high-alpine views.
places.goodToKnow: Summer-only: reopens ~20 Jun 2026 and runs through late Sep; closed all winter/spring. Bring a passport or ID card (you cross the Italian border). Runs only in favourable weather; short daily windows - check same-day status.
places.address: Aiguille du Midi summit station, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Montenvers Rack Railway & Mer de Glace
places.area: Chamonix (departs from Montenvers station beside the main SNCF train station)
places.near: Montenvers station is a 2-minute walk from Chamonix's main train station in the town centre.
places.order: Round-trip Montenvers train ticket including the gondola down to the ice cave; allow 2.5-3 hours. Combine with the Grand Balcon Nord hike for a one-way descent option.
places.notes: Combine with the Grotte de Glace (ice cave) below for the full experience.
places.famousFor: A charming red cogwheel train (running since 1908) that climbs 900 m in ~20 minutes to a grand balcony overlooking the Mer de Glace, France's largest glacier.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 season ~1 Jun-1 Nov; last ice-cave departure ~3:00 pm. Industrial action may disrupt service from early July for an indefinite period - check status before travelling. On-site glaciorium and mountain-fauna museum add context.
places.address: 35 Place de la Mer de Glace, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Grotte de Glace (Mer de Glace Ice Cave)
places.near: Reached via the Montenvers train, then a short gondola down toward the glacier, then a staircase.
places.order: Included with the Montenvers round-trip ticket; take the small gondola down from Montenvers, then descend the metal staircase (~500+ steps round trip) to the cave.
places.notes: Bring a light jacket; the cave stays near freezing.
places.famousFor: A tunnel and rooms carved by hand into the living glacier each year, lit in blue and dotted with ice sculptures - you literally walk inside the Mer de Glace.
places.goodToKnow: Open with the Montenvers season (~Jun-Nov 2026); the many steps make it a workout for the ice cave. Poignant markers show how far the glacier has retreated - a striking, Instagrammable and educational stop. Same July industrial-action caveat as the train.
places.address: Mer de Glace, below Montenvers, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Le Brévent Cable Car
places.area: Chamonix (Plan Praz gondola departs from town centre)
places.near: The Planpraz gondola departs a short uphill walk from Chamonix town centre (near the sports centre).
places.order: Round-trip via the Planpraz gondola then the Brévent cable car; enjoy the Le Panoramique restaurant terrace or hike toward Bel-Lachat / Aiguillette des Houches in summer.
places.notes: Top take-off point for many tandem paragliding flights.
places.famousFor: The best head-on view of Mont Blanc and the Aiguille du Midi from across the valley (2,525 m) - a photographer's and paraglider's favourite, with a 360-degree Alpine panorama.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 season ~13 Jun-13 Sep. Top summit cable car can close for wind/maintenance while the lower Planpraz gondola still runs. Best light on Mont Blanc is afternoon/sunset from here.

places.name: La Flégère Gondola & L'Index Chairlift
places.near: Gondola base station in Les Praz village, ~2.5 km north of Chamonix centre (bus or drive; parking on-site).
places.order: Round-trip Flégère gondola (approx. 25 EUR adult) plus the Index chairlift if you want to shorten the Lac Blanc climb; family pass available.
places.famousFor: The classic launch point for the Lac Blanc hike, with sweeping views over the Mont Blanc massif and the Chamonix golf course on the way up; the Index chairlift lifts you to 2,595 m.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 season ~20 Jun-late Sep; last ascents mid-late afternoon (Index closes earlier than Flégère). Ideal for the Grand Balcon Sud and Lac Blanc. Combines with the Brévent side for a full 'Aiguilles Rouges' day.
places.address: 1249 Route de la Flégère, Les Praz, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Lac Blanc & Lacs des Chéserys Hike
places.area: Aiguilles Rouges, above Les Praz / La Flégère
places.near: Start from the top of the La Flégère gondola in Les Praz; upper trailhead reachable via the Index chairlift.
places.order: Take the Flégère gondola (and optionally the Index chairlift) to cut the climb, then walk to Lacs des Chéserys for the reflection shots and up to Lac Blanc (2,352 m). Descend via the Grand Balcon Sud for a loop.
places.notes: For reflections, walk to the far side of Lacs des Chéserys before the final climb.
places.famousFor: Chamonix's most photographed hike: turquoise high-alpine lakes that mirror Mont Blanc and the Aiguilles on calm days - arguably the valley's ultimate Instagram shot.
places.setting: Signature day hike / alpine lakes / photography
places.goodToKnow: Best July to mid-September (snow lingers on the trail before late June and after mid-Sep). Go early morning or evening for calm water and light. Sturdy shoes needed; the Index approach has loose scree. Lift-assisted, it is doable for fit families.

places.name: Grand Balcon Nord Hike (Plan de l'Aiguille to Montenvers)
places.area: North face balcony, Aiguille du Midi to Mer de Glace
places.near: Start at Plan de l'Aiguille, the mid-station of the Aiguille du Midi cable car from Chamonix; finish at Montenvers.
places.order: Ride the Aiguille du Midi cable car to Plan de l'Aiguille (2,317 m), hike the balcony to Montenvers (add the Signal Forbes detour, 2,198 m, for the best glacier overlook), then take the Montenvers train down.
places.notes: One of the best value 'two icons in one day' outings. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A spectacular high-balcony trail linking the Aiguille du Midi's mid-station to the Mer de Glace, with in-your-face views of the granite spires and the glacier - and an optional Signal Forbes viewpoint over the ice.
places.goodToKnow: Summer trail (roughly late Jun-Sep, snow-dependent); easiest hiked downhill from Plan de l'Aiguille to Montenvers. Uses two lifts (cable car up, train down) so plan ticketing accordingly. Marmots and eagles often seen.

places.name: Grand Balcon Sud Hike
places.area: Aiguilles Rouges side, La Flégère to Planpraz/Brévent
places.near: Access from the top of the La Flégère gondola (Les Praz) or the Planpraz gondola (Chamonix centre).
places.order: Link the La Flégère and Planpraz lift stations along the balcony (either direction); flat-ish and family-friendly with constant Mont Blanc views. Combine with a Lac Blanc extension.
places.notes: Great option for families and less-experienced walkers wanting big views. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A gentler high-level traverse along the sunny Aiguilles Rouges side (part of the Tour du Mont Blanc) with a continuous head-on panorama of the entire Mont Blanc massif opposite.
places.setting: Balcony day hike / panorama / Tour du Mont Blanc
places.goodToKnow: Summer season (lifts ~late Jun-Sep). Being sun-exposed, bring sun protection and water. One of the most rewarding easy-moderate hikes for the effort thanks to lift assistance at both ends.
places.address: Grand Balcon Sud, Aiguilles Rouges, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Plan de l'Aiguille (Aiguille du Midi Mid-Station)
places.near: Mid-station of the Aiguille du Midi cable car from Chamonix; stop here on the way down.
places.order: Buy the Aiguille du Midi ticket only as far as Plan de l'Aiguille (much cheaper than the summit) and lunch or picnic at the Refuge du Plan de l'Aiguille; a 10-minute walk from the station.
places.notes: A relaxed, family-friendly way to sample the high mountains.
places.famousFor: A gentler alternative to the full summit ascent - a flowery alpine terrace at the foot of the granite needles with a cosy refuge, superb Mont Blanc views and marmot sightings.
places.goodToKnow: Accessible in the Aiguille du Midi season (~mid-Jun-mid-Sep). A good low-altitude option for those who find 3,842 m too high; also the start of the Grand Balcon Nord. Refuge picnic packs available.

places.name: Tramway du Mont-Blanc & Nid d'Aigle
places.near: Departs from Le Fayet (581 m) or Saint-Gervais; ~45 min drive or train from Chamonix to Saint-Gervais-Le Fayet.
places.order: Round-trip to the Nid d'Aigle terminus; the newly extended 2026 platform delivers you right at the refuge for easy glacier-edge walks and family-friendly viewing.
places.notes: A worthwhile half-day trip from Chamonix for railway and glacier lovers. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: France's highest rack railway, climbing from Le Fayet/Saint-Gervais to the Nid d'Aigle at 2,372 m - the gateway to the Bionnassay glacier and the classic Mont Blanc ascent route.
places.goodToKnow: Summer service (roughly late Jun-Sep); the 280 m extension to the new Nid d'Aigle terminus opened 27 Jun 2026, making the top far more accessible. Book ahead in peak summer. Department took over operations in May 2026.
places.address: Gare du Fayet, 40 Avenue de la Gare, 74190 Saint-Gervais-les-Bains

places.name: Bellevue Cable Car (Les Houches)
places.near: Base station in Les Houches, ~8 km southwest of Chamonix (bus or drive; parking on-site).
places.order: Round-trip Bellevue cable car (approx. 24 EUR adult, family pass available); accepts mountain bikes. Combine with a walk to the Col de Voza or the tramway.
places.famousFor: A quick lift from Les Houches to the sunny Bellevue plateau (1,800 m), a launchpad for family walks, the Tour du Mont Blanc, and a connection to the Tramway du Mont-Blanc.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 summer season ~6 Jun-13 Sep. Quieter and cheaper than the Chamonix lifts, with lovely pastoral scenery - great for families. Links to the Tramway du Mont-Blanc at Bellevue station.

places.name: Prarion Gondola (Les Houches)
places.near: Base station in Les Houches (near the village); ~8 km from Chamonix.
places.order: Round-trip Prarion gondola; walk the easy ridge for panoramas, or descend the singletrack MTB trails through forest and meadow. New EVO panoramic cabins from 2026.
places.famousFor: A family-favourite gondola to the Prarion plateau with relaxed 360-degree views over the Mont Blanc range and gentle summit walks - plus a dedicated downhill mountain-bike area.
places.goodToKnow: 2026 summer ~20 Jun-30 Aug (season shifted a week earlier for a modernisation project); daily ~9:15 am-5 pm. Ideal for families, beginner walkers and downhill bikers.

places.name: Parc de Merlet (Wildlife Park)
places.area: Les Houches (1,500 m above the valley)
places.near: Above Les Houches; reached by a winding mountain road then a short walk, or a scenic uphill hike from the village (~1-1.5 hr).
places.order: General admission (approx. 9 EUR adult / 6 EUR child); allow 2-3 hours including the gentle loop walk. Go early or late for the most active animals.
places.notes: One of the best family + nature + Instagram combos in the valley.
places.famousFor: A magical mid-mountain park where ibex, chamois, marmots, mouflon and deer roam in semi-liberty against a jaw-dropping Mont Blanc backdrop - superb for kids and wildlife photography.
places.setting: Wildlife park / family nature outing / viewpoint
places.goodToKnow: 2026 season ~1 May-30 Sep (daily 9:30 am-7:30 pm in Jul-Aug; Tue-Sun in shoulder months, closed Mondays outside peak). No dogs; wear proper shoes for the trail. Wildlife is free-roaming, so sightings vary.

places.name: Chamonix Luge Alpine Coaster (Les Planards)
places.near: At the Les Planards ski area, a short walk from Chamonix town centre and the Montenvers train station.
places.order: Single or multi-ride tickets (no reservation needed); kids can ride with an adult. Combine with the park's dozen-plus summer attractions.
places.notes: Great rainy-day-adjacent option and a hit with children.
places.famousFor: A 1,300 m rail toboggan with 540-degree spirals, banked turns and jumps - the flagship ride of the Chamonix Leisure Park and a guaranteed family adrenaline hit.
places.goodToKnow: Open almost all year (annual closure ~Nov to mid-Dec); 2026 summer daily 15 Jun-13 Sep (10:30 am-7:30 pm in peak). All-weather; runs rain or shine.
places.address: 351 Chemin du Pied du Grépon, Les Planards, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Tandem Paragliding over Chamonix (Planpraz / Brévent)
places.near: Meet operator in town; ride the Planpraz gondola to the take-off site above Chamonix.
places.order: Discovery tandem flight from Planpraz (~20-30 min air time, ~1 hr total); longer-duration flights (45-60 min) available. Add GoPro footage. Reputable operators: FlyMB, Absolute Chamonix, Wingover, Evolution 2, Chamonix Parapente.
places.notes: Bucket-list experience for couples and adventurous families; compare operators before booking.
places.famousFor: Considered one of Europe's most beautiful tandem flights - a ~1,000 m glide over the Chamonix valley with the entire Mont Blanc massif spread beneath you.
places.goodToKnow: Weather-dependent (flights cancel in wind/rain); book ahead in summer and keep flexible dates. Child flights available with weight limits. Gondola ticket for take-off usually separate.
places.address: Take-off at Planpraz (top of the Planpraz gondola), 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: QC Terme Chamonix Mont-Blanc (Luxury Spa)
places.near: On the Promenade du Fori in Chamonix (Les Pèlerins area), a short drive or bus from the centre; parking available.
places.order: Full-day or evening 'access' ticket (includes pools, saunas, steam rooms and relaxation areas); add a massage or treatment. Book in advance and aim for off-peak times.
places.notes: Ideal luxury/couples add-on, especially on a cloudy day.
places.famousFor: An indulgent multi-sensory spa with indoor and outdoor thermal pools, saunas, steam rooms and waterfalls, all framed by Mont Blanc views - the valley's premier luxury wellness escape.
places.goodToKnow: Open year-round; can get crowded and noisy at peak times, so go early or on a weekday for a calmer, more romantic experience. Bring swimwear; robe/towel packages available. Adults-oriented (age limits apply) - better for couples than young kids.

places.name: Chamonix Town Centre & Église Saint-Michel
places.near: The heart of Chamonix; walkable from the train station and all central hotels.
places.order: A leisurely stroll around Place Balmat and Rue du Docteur Paccard; photograph the church and statues; browse mountaineering boutiques and patisseries.
places.notes: The classic, no-cost Chamonix wander for all travellers. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: A postcard-perfect Alpine town of pastel façades, flower-lined balconies and café terraces along the Arve, crowned by the Église Saint-Michel and the pioneering Balmat-Saussure and Dr Paccard statues pointing to Mont Blanc.
places.goodToKnow: Free and open year-round. Come early morning or near sunset for the best light on the peaks behind town and quieter streets. The riverside and old bridges are especially photogenic.

places.name: Musée Alpin / Mont-Blanc Museum
places.near: Central Chamonix, a short walk from Place Balmat.
places.order: General admission; a good ~1-1.5 hr rainy-day or culture stop, especially for history and mountaineering buffs.
places.notes: Great wet-weather and cultural option once reopened. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: The story of Chamonix and Mont Blanc - from the first 1786 ascent through the golden age of alpinism to the birth of winter sports - told via ~15,000 artefacts.
places.goodToKnow: The museum has been closed for renovation and is scheduled to reopen as the 'Mont-Blanc Museum' in July 2026 (opening hours ~10 am-1 pm and 2-6 pm, closed Tuesdays). Confirm the reopening date before visiting. (Needs verification)

places.name: Gorges de la Diosaz
places.near: In Servoz, ~10-15 min drive from Chamonix (also reachable by train to Servoz then a short walk/taxi).
places.order: Paid entry for the easy out-and-back gorge circuit (~1.5-2 hrs round trip); good for children with care near the railings.
places.notes: A true hidden gem away from the main lift crowds. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A dramatic, shaded canyon walk on suspended walkways past five thundering waterfalls - a refreshing, lesser-known family gem, especially welcome on hot days.
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal - typically open ~May/Jun-Sep (closed in winter/spring due to ice and rockfall); check current dates. Cool and damp inside; bring a light layer. Inexpensive and shaded.

places.name: Cascade du Dard
places.area: Chamonix (south of town, toward Les Bossons)
places.near: South of Chamonix near the Bossons; on foot from town or a short drive to the trailhead.
places.order: Short walk (~45 min from Chamonix centre or a shorter approach from the nearby parking); pause at the Buvette de la Cascade du Dard for a snack.
places.notes: Easy hidden-gem outing close to town.
places.famousFor: A 20 m waterfall in a leafy setting with views toward the Bossons glacier and Mont Blanc - an easy, free walk that most people miss.
places.goodToKnow: Best in late spring/summer when flow is strong and the path is snow-free. Family-friendly and free. The buvette is seasonal.

places.name: Lac des Gaillands
places.near: About 20 min walk southwest of Chamonix centre (or a short drive/bus); parking nearby.
places.order: A short, flat lakeside loop; watch climbers on the Gaillands crag or picnic with the Mont Blanc reflection. Combine with the adjacent Accro'Park.
places.notes: Underrated, easy Mont Blanc photo stop.
places.famousFor: A tranquil valley-floor lake that mirrors Mont Blanc and the Bossons glacier - an easy, buggy-friendly stroll and a favourite spot for climbers on the adjacent cliffs.
places.setting: Valley lake / easy family walk / Mont Blanc reflection
places.goodToKnow: Free and accessible year-round (best reflections on calm, clear mornings). Very easy - ideal for toddlers and grandparents. No lift required.

places.name: Accro'Park des Gaillands (Treetop Adventure & Ziplines)
places.near: By Lac des Gaillands, ~20 min walk from Chamonix centre; parking nearby.
places.order: Choose courses by difficulty (kids' routes up to high adult circuits); operated by the Compagnie des Guides de Chamonix. Allow 2-3 hours.
places.notes: Top adventure pick for families with older kids and teens.
places.famousFor: The Chamonix valley's largest adventure park - 4-6 courses and 100+ elements through the forest, with monster ziplines, Tarzan swings and a flying skateboard.
places.setting: Treetop adventure park / ziplines / family adventure
places.goodToKnow: Summer season (roughly late spring-early autumn); weather-dependent and busy on sunny afternoons. Minimum height/age for higher courses. Book ahead in peak weeks.
places.address: Lac des Gaillands, Route des Gaillands, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: White-Water Rafting on the Arve
places.near: Operators meet in/around Chamonix and transfer you to the put-in on the Arve.
places.order: Half-day guided raft trip; family/beginner sessions available (typically from age 8). Operators include Mont Blanc Rafting, Yellow Rafting, Evolution 2 and the Compagnie des Guides.
places.notes: Best adventure-water option for active families and couples. (location needs verification)
places.famousFor: An exhilarating descent of the glacier-fed Arve (Class III) beneath the Mont Blanc peaks - the valley's headline warm-weather water adventure.
places.goodToKnow: Season ~1 Apr-15 Oct 2026, daily. Cold glacial water - wetsuits provided; book ahead in summer. Minimum age applies. Canyoning and hydrospeed also offered by the same operators.
places.address: Chamonix Valley (Arve river put-in; meet at operator base in Chamonix)

places.name: Lac Cornu & Lacs Noirs Hike
places.near: Access from the Planpraz gondola (Chamonix) or the Flégère/Index lifts (Les Praz).
places.order: Hike from the top of the Planpraz gondola (or the Index side) to Lac Cornu, with the nearby Lacs Noirs a 10-minute detour. A moderate, less-crowded outing.
places.notes: A genuine hidden-gem hike for those wanting solitude and ibex.
places.famousFor: A quieter, wilder alternative to Lac Blanc - a rugged basin of dark alpine lakes ringed by cliffs where bands of wild ibex are often seen clinging to the rock.
places.setting: High-alpine lakes hike / wild scenery / ibex
places.goodToKnow: Snow-free window is short (the lakes are frozen much of the year) - best mid-summer to early autumn. More rugged terrain than Lac Blanc; sure footing needed. Great for wildlife spotting.

places.name: Bois du Bouchet (Woods, Playground & Riverside Trails)
places.area: Chamonix (northeast of centre, toward Les Praz)
places.near: Just northeast of Chamonix centre, an easy walk or cycle; parking available.
places.order: Stroll or cycle the woodland loop to Les Praz and back along the river; let kids loose at the playground; try the orienteering course. Snack stops at La Crèmerie or Le Robinson.
places.notes: Perfect low-key family spot between big mountain days.
places.famousFor: A flat, shaded forest park beside the Arve with playgrounds, picnic tables, cycle paths and buggy-friendly trails - the go-to free family green space in Chamonix.
places.setting: Woodland park / family playground / easy walking & cycling
places.goodToKnow: Free and open year-round (playground and trails best in summer; Nordic ski loops in winter). Ideal for toddlers, buggies and rest days. Plenty of shade for hot afternoons.

places.name: Le Paradis des Praz (Children's Adventure Playground)
places.near: About 2.5 km from Chamonix centre in Les Praz, along a small river; walk, cycle or short drive.
places.order: Free-play in the outdoor area (aimed at ages ~2-7); pack a picnic and swimwear/spare clothes for the water play.
places.notes: Charming toddler-friendly gem near La Flégère. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: A riverside natural adventure playground for young children with water channels feeding a little lake, aerial slides, dinghy boats, swings and pony rides.
places.goodToKnow: Summer-oriented and weather-dependent; best for younger kids. Pony rides and some activities may be seasonal/paid. Lovely Mont Blanc backdrop. (Needs verification)

places.name: Salon de thé Pâtissier chamonix
places.near: Central Chamonix
places.order: Macarons
places.notes: الذ ماكرون
places.famousFor: Macarons and French pastries with a tea salon
places.goodToKnow: Likely 'Côté Macarons' salon de thé; verify the exact shop on your pin

places.name: Moody Coffee Roasters
places.near: Chamonix Sud, a 3-4 min walk from the Aiguille du Midi cable-car station; central and walkable
places.order: Cappuccino or flat white with a cinnamon roll; brownie, cookies, gluten-free/vegan cakes
places.notes: Flagship specialty-coffee destination; couples & coffee lovers.
places.famousFor: The valley's benchmark specialty-coffee roaster; small-batch beans roasted on-site (Colombia, Ethiopia, Kenya, Brazil, Peru) and legendary cinnamon rolls
places.goodToKnow: Tiny, minimalist grab-and-go space (limited seating). Typically Tue-Sat ~8:00-15:00, closed Sun-Mon; hours vary seasonally, so verify. Founded 2014 (roasting), café opened 2017.
places.address: 195 Avenue de l'Aiguille du Midi, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc

places.name: Couloir
places.near: Central Chamonix on Avenue Michel Croz, near the train station; easy walk from town centre
places.order: Cappuccino and a pastry; cinnamon rolls
places.notes: Best specialty option for sitting down; couples & hidden-gem feel.
places.famousFor: Sister café to Moody with a warm, Instagram-worthy interior and chocolate-forward espresso; roomier and better for lingering than Moody
places.goodToKnow: Run by the Moody team; ample seating, good for coffee dates or remote work. Cozy, photogenic space.

places.name: Shoukâ - Torréfacteur d'Altitude
places.near: Main pedestrian street (Rue du Docteur Paccard) in the town centre; very walkable
places.order: Filter or espresso coffee, a hot chocolate, single-origin chocolate bars, freshly made cakes
places.notes: Standout for chocolate lovers as well as coffee; family-friendly.
places.famousFor: France's pioneering double 'bean-to-cup' coffee + 'bean-to-bar' chocolate house (founded by Nathalie & Christian Duperrier); both roasted/made on site
places.setting: Specialty coffee roaster & bean-to-bar chocolate café
places.goodToKnow: Indoor + outdoor seating plus a shop to buy coffee and chocolate; runs chocolate factory tours/tastings. Great for gifts.

places.name: Green Gorilla Coffee
places.cuisine: Specialty coffee, wellness bowls, health food
places.near: Central Chamonix inside Snell Sports on Galerie Alpina; walkable from the main square
places.order: Cappuccino; wellness/acai-style bowls and healthy bites
places.notes: Quirky hidden gem for a quick quality coffee.
places.famousFor: Genuinely good specialty coffee tucked inside the Snell Sports shop; a fun 'hidden' spot with properly made cappuccinos
places.goodToKnow: Casual, practical setting with seating and workspace; easy to combine with gear shopping. Confirm current opening days.

places.name: Café Tartine
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue Joseph Vallot; short walk from the church square
places.order: A signature tartine, carrot cake or a fruit tart; coffee/brunch plates
places.notes: One of the most Instagrammable spots in town; couples & brunch.
places.famousFor: Beautiful, bright, design-forward interior; house-baked tartines (open-faced sandwiches) on thick homemade bread
places.goodToKnow: Open year-round; Sun-Thu ~7:00-19:00, Fri-Sat ~7:00-22:00. Vegetarian/vegan options. Very photogenic. Coffee quality can be a touch inconsistent per some reviews.

places.name: Aux Petits Gourmands
places.area: Chamonix Centre (Rue du Docteur Paccard); second café in Chamonix Sud
places.cuisine: French pastry, viennoiserie, chocolate, tea & coffee
places.near: Main shop on Rue du Docteur Paccard in the town centre; smaller café in Chamonix Sud
places.order: Chocolate or coffee éclair, raspberry/berry tartelette, viennoiserie, hot chocolate; enjoy on the sunny terrace
places.notes: Classic French patisserie & salon de thé; great with kids.
places.famousFor: Long-standing local favourite with jewel-like pastries at fair prices and handmade chocolates, including the '4810' mini-Mont-Blanc chocolates
places.goodToKnow: Main shop open daily ~07:30-19:30; Chamonix Sud branch has seasonal hours. Beautiful window displays; family-friendly.

places.name: Chalet 4810
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue Joseph Vallot; walkable from the town centre
places.order: '4810' Mont-Blanc chocolates, a macaron or shared pastry, and an aromatic tea in the upstairs salon
places.notes: Cozy mountain salon de thé; couples & families.
places.famousFor: Elegant wooden chalet with a cozy upstairs tea salon; the signature '4810' chocolates shaped like mini Mont Blancs and a balcony overlooking the street
places.goodToKnow: Everything is homemade (viennoiserie, pastries, macarons, chocolates, quiches). Warm chalet-elegant decor with crystals; very Instagrammable.

places.name: Pâtisserie Richard
places.cuisine: French pastry, cakes, chocolates, ice cream
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue du Docteur Paccard; easy town-centre walk
places.order: The 'Mont Blanc' (chestnut-cream dessert), a seasonal pastry, ice cream or a fresh milkshake
places.notes: Best-in-town for classic French dessert; families.
places.famousFor: One of the best addresses in town for dessert cakes and refined pâtisserie; everything homemade from bread to complex cakes
places.goodToKnow: Elegant colourful window displays; also does custom/birthday cakes. Great grab-a-cake stop.

places.name: Pin Pin Bakery
places.near: Central Chamonix on Avenue Ravanel le Rouge; walkable from the centre
places.order: Sourdough loaf, a buttery croissant, pistachio roll, lemon-poppyseed cake or chocolate babka with a coffee
places.notes: Trendy hidden gem for bread and pastry lovers. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: One of the newest arrivals on the pastry scene: small-batch, slow-fermentation baking with creative weekly specials
places.goodToKnow: Understated, artisan style; great for a morning pastry-and-coffee or a chalet-breakfast loaf. Menu rotates weekly.

places.name: Gâteau & Cacao
places.near: Central Chamonix on Rue Joseph Vallot; town-centre walk
places.order: Handmade pralines and truffles, delicate mousses/pastries, signature chocolate bars
places.notes: For chocolate & dessert lovers; couples. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Elegant chocolate boutique founded by artisan chocolatier Benoît; fine craftsmanship and seasonal collections
places.goodToKnow: More a refined boutique than a sit-down café; ideal for a sweet treat or gift. Beautiful presentation.

places.name: Bighorn Bistro & Bakery
places.cuisine: Craft coffee, homemade bakery, American-style brunch
places.near: Heart of Chamonix Sud, near the Aiguille du Midi cable-car; walkable
places.order: Craft coffee (Lavazza), freshly baked cinnamon rolls and buttermilk pancakes at Sunday brunch
places.notes: Family-friendly brunch; go for the daytime bakery, not the evening bar.
places.famousFor: Family-run spot with a cozy lodge interior, its own daily-changing bakery and a popular American Sunday brunch
places.goodToKnow: Sunday brunch ~10:30-14:30 (takeaway available); evenings the bistro serves burgers/steaks and does serve alcohol, but daytime café/bakery/brunch is the draw. Hours vary seasonally.

places.name: Bluebird Café
places.area: Chamonix Sud (opposite Aiguille du Midi lift)
places.cuisine: Coffee, cooked breakfast, sandwiches, home baking
places.near: Chamonix Sud, directly opposite the Aiguille du Midi lift station; ideal pre-mountain stop
places.order: The Blue Bird breakfast, coffee, wraps/sandwiches and home baking
places.notes: Great fuel-up before the cable car; families & groups.
places.famousFor: Small, well-loved locals' spot for a filling breakfast right by the cable-car; its 'Blue Bird' full-breakfast
places.goodToKnow: Open daily ~08:00-15:00; English-speaking staff make ordering easy. Small, so it can fill up at peak breakfast time.
places.address: Chamonix Sud, Gare Routière Chamonix Sud, Les Bossons, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Bonneville, Haute-Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France métropolitaine, 74400, France

places.name: L'Atelier Café
places.cuisine: Breakfast, brunch, crepes, salads, hot chocolate
places.near: Central Chamonix beside the Arve river; short walk from the main square
places.order: Thick hot chocolate, a crepe or brunch plate; salads and soups for a light lunch
places.notes: Scenic all-day café; families & couples.
places.famousFor: Riverside terrace with mountain views and famously thick, rich hot chocolate
places.goodToKnow: Open daily year-round ~08:30-21:00; sunny riverside terrace. Relaxed, good for families.
places.address: L’Atelier Café, 8, Quai d'Arve, Les Plans, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Bonneville, Haute-Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France métropolitaine, 74400, France

places.name: Arctic Juice Café
places.cuisine: Organic coffee, matcha, cold-pressed juices, brunch bowls
places.near: On the main pedestrian high street in the town centre; very central
places.order: Organic coffee or matcha, a cold-pressed juice, avocado toast or a 'soul bowl'
places.notes: Instagrammable healthy brunch; couples & wellness-minded travellers.
places.famousFor: Trendy, health-focused café on the main street; bright brunch bowls and cold-pressed juices
places.goodToKnow: Popular light/healthy option and photogenic bowls; good for a lighter breakfast or post-hike recharge.
places.address: Chamonix Centre, Allée Recteur Payot, Les Plans, Les Bossons, Chamonix-Mont-Blanc, Bonneville, Haute-Savoie, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France métropolitaine, 74400, France

places.name: Shoukâ / Tête à Thé (Argentière)
places.cuisine: Homemade cakes, specialty teas, coffee, light vegetarian food
places.near: Argentière Old Town (~10 km up-valley from Chamonix), opposite the old church; ~300 m from Argentière train station
places.order: Homemade cake or cookies with a specialty tea, cappuccino or latte; veggie soup/quiche for lunch
places.notes: Hidden-gem tea room; families & couples wanting a slower stop. Note: the venue name is 'Tête à Thé'.
places.famousFor: Charming village tea room opposite the old church; owner-baked cakes and a wide tea selection with gluten-free options
places.goodToKnow: Everything freshly made and vegetarian; gluten-free choices. Cozy, quiet village setting away from the crowds.

places.name: Le Café Comptoir
places.cuisine: Coffee, homemade Alpine food, brunch-style daytime menu
places.near: Vallorcine (~15 km from Chamonix), by the village train station at the foot of the Forêt Verte slope; reachable by the Mont-Blanc Express train
places.order: A coffee and homemade cake by day; for a meal, the house 'La Mystère' melted-reblochon specialty
places.notes: Scenic hidden gem up the valley; families & couples on a train day-trip.
places.famousFor: Warm chalet-meets-atelier café in the farming village of Vallorcine, committed to local, fresh, homemade produce
places.goodToKnow: Opens from 9:00 (Sun 9-17; Wed-Sat & holidays 9-22). Great scenic day-trip café stop; evenings it's a full Savoyard restaurant (alcohol served then).
places.address: 69 Route de la Forêt Verte, Le Plan Envers, 74660 Vallorcine

places.name: Buvette du Plan de l'Aiguille
places.area: Plan de l'Aiguille (2,207 m, above Chamonix)
places.cuisine: Sandwiches, paninis, homemade tarts, hot & cold drinks
places.near: Take the Aiguille du Midi cable car to the Plan de l'Aiguille mid-station (2,207 m); it's a short walk from the lift, also on the Grand Balcon Nord trail
places.order: A coffee or hot chocolate with a homemade tart or a panini/sandwich on the terrace
places.notes: Most Instagrammable Mont-Blanc-view café; couples & families with the cable car. (Needs verification)
places.famousFor: Spectacular sun-terrace perched on a ridge with front-row Mont Blanc massif and Bossons Glacier views; a classic scenic coffee/snack stop
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal (roughly summer/good-weather months) and weather-dependent; small indoor area plus a large terrace. Check the lift is running and the buvette is open before going.

places.name: Le Fournil Chamoniard
places.area: Chamonix Sud (Avenue de l'Aiguille du Midi) + Les Praz branch
places.near: Chamonix Sud on Avenue de l'Aiguille du Midi (near the cable car), plus a branch toward Les Praz; walkable/short bus
places.order: Almond croissant with chocolate chips, a Croque Monsieur or baguette sandwich, and the 'Viking' bread
places.notes: Reliable no-frills viennoiserie stop; families & budget-minded.
places.famousFor: Authentic French bakery with everything baked on-premises; famed for its baguette sandwiches and almond-chocolate croissant
places.goodToKnow: Quality without frills; great value and generous portions for a grab-and-go pastry or picnic-worthy sandwich before the mountain.
places.address: 204 Avenue de l'Aiguille du Midi, 74400 Chamonix-Mont-Blanc
